title: Best Original Screenplay: Ranking Every Nominee of the 98th Academy Awards
text: Films Fatale's ranking of the 98th Academy Awards Best Original Screenplay nominees: 1. "Sentimental Value" (Eskil Vogt, Joachim Trier) - Ranked first for its "poetically rich" characters and "Bergman-esque" dialogue that feels both realistic and profound. 2. "It Was Just an Accident" (Jafar Panahi and collaborators) - Praised as "one of the best written films of 2025," this moral thriller features exceptional dialogue in pivotal sequences. 3. "Sinners" (Ryan Coogler) - Commended for seamlessly blending vampire horror with Mississippi Delta drama and Black musical history, though some concepts feel familiar. 4. "Marty Supreme" (Ronald Bronstein, Josh Safdie) - Features hilarious dialogue and unique characters, though some storylines remain unresolved, affecting its screenplay quality. 5. "Blue Moon" (Robert Kaplow) - Despite strong character work capturing songwriter Lorenz Hart's vulnerability, it "had more to offer" compared to competitors. Prediction: The critic expects Ryan Coogler's "Sinners" to win, as it's seen as a major contender that could propel the film toward Best Picture success.
